Job description

Software Engineer – Level 2

Location: Remote (Arizona Residents Only)
Type: Full-Time
Start Date: Flexible
Experience: 2+ years professional software development experience

About the Role

The Arizona Software Group (ASG) is a small, high-performing team that builds custom software used by real organizations around the world. Our work powers mission-driven nonprofits and other large businesses, and the systems we build are relied on every day.

We are looking for a Level 2 Software Engineer who can operate independently in a remote environment, take ownership of their work, and approach problems with curiosity, discipline, and integrity.

In a small team like ours, every developer matters. The right person will not only write solid code but will also think critically about problems, communicate clearly, and take pride in delivering high-quality work.  You'll wear many hats, communicate with clients, participate in architecture decisions, etc.

Technical skills are important, but character, work ethic, and attention to detail matter even more.

What You’ll Do

Design, develop, and maintain business-critical software applications
Take ownership of features and systems from design through deployment
Work independently in a remote environment while collaborating with a small development team
Write clean, reliable, maintainable code with strong attention to detail
Troubleshoot issues and solve complex problems across application and data layers
Participate in code reviews and contribute thoughtful feedback
Help improve existing systems while contributing to new development
Communicate clearly about progress, risks, and solutions

What We’re Looking For

2+ years of professional software development experience
Ability to work independently and manage responsibilities in a remote environment
Strong attention to detail and commitment to producing high-quality work
A strong personal work ethic and a high standard of professional integrity
A natural problem solver who enjoys figuring out difficult technical challenges
Clear communication and collaboration skills
Ability to learn new technologies and systems quickly
Must reside in the state of Arizona
Authorization to work in the United States (Mandatory)

Technical Environment

Our work primarily involves web-based business applications using technologies such as:

.NET / C#
SQL Server
JavaScript and modern web tools
REST APIs

Experience with these technologies is helpful but not required if you have a strong foundation and the ability to learn quickly.

Why Join ASG

Join a small, highly capable engineering team where your work truly matters. At ASG, you’ll build real-world software used by national organizations that rely on our systems every day. You’ll have the autonomy and trust that comes with a remote-first culture, the opportunity to solve meaningful problems for real clients, and the ability to grow your skills while contributing to software that has a genuine impact. In a small team like ours, your ideas, effort, and craftsmanship will be visible and valued.

About the employer
The Arizona Software Group

Internet & Software

Follow
1-10 employees

Tucson, AZ

At The Arizona Software Group (ASG), we build custom software that makes a difference.  

We’re a boutique team of engineers, designers, and problem solvers who craft business-critical systems for mission-driven organizations. Our clients range from national non-profits to public companies—and we take pride in developing clean, maintainable software that drives real-world results.

We don’t just write code—we architect solutions. Our stack is Microsoft-focused (.NET Core, SQL Server, Razor Pages, Azure), and we value clarity, simplicity, and thoughtful design. From rebuilding legacy systems to designing brand-new platforms, we tackle a wide range of challenges that keep things interesting.

We offer:

Mentorship & growth: You’ll work directly with senior devs who love to teach and learn.
Real impact: The code you write will go into production—used by thousands of people across the country.
A strong engineering culture: We care about testing, architecture, and doing things right.
Remote flexibility: Our team is fully remote, with optional in-person meetups and events.

If you’re smart, motivated, and ready to contribute to meaningful projects from day one—we’d love to meet you.

Learn more about The Arizona Software Group
